name: Calibration Data
oneliner: A small dataset (~128 samples) run through the model to collect activation statistics needed by quantization methods to set scale factors.
explanation: Some methods need to observe actual activation ranges (which depend on input data) to set quantization parameters. Standard: 128 samples $\times$ 2048 tokens from C4 or WikiText-2 (established by GPTQ paper). Required by: GPTQ (Hessian computation), AWQ (activation importance), SmoothQuant (smoothing factors), static quantization. NOT required by: simple RTN, dynamic quantization, HQQ. Domain-matched calibration (e.g., code for code models) yields slightly better results.
fundamentals: For layer Y=XW+b: calibration records per-layer activation stats. Approaches: min/max (simple but outlier-sensitive), percentile (clips outliers), MSE-optimal (minimizes reconstruction error), entropy/KL-based (TensorRT). For GPTQ: calibration computes Hessian H = 2XX^T, used to solve layer-wise optimization min_Q $\|\|WX - QX\|\|^2$.

resources.label: White Paper on Neural Network Quantization
